screens.doc: α.3 — primary tab: user's screens (default landing).

create_unavailable.text: Can't create a circle yet — the app is still starting.

members.doc: Member count on a circle tile; {{count}} interpolated.

tile_unread.doc: Accessible label for the unread badge on a circle tile (P6.3).

tile_proposals.doc: Accessible label for the voorstellen badge on a circle tile (P6.2 #341).

pin.doc: β.5 — context-menu item: float this kring to the top of its section.

mute.doc: β.5 — context-menu item: silence this kring (toggles per-member chatOff override).

unmute.doc: β.5 — context-menu item: re-enable chat for this kring.

settings.doc: β.5 — context-menu item: open this kring's settings screen.

leave.doc: β.5 — context-menu item: leave this kring (dispatches /leave-group).

household.doc: β.3 — launcher section header for kind='household' circles.

buurt.doc: β.3 — launcher section header for kind='buurt' circles.

vriendenkring.doc: β.3 — launcher section header for kind='vriendenkring' circles.

other.doc: β.3 — launcher section header for circles whose kind isn't household/buurt/vriendenkring.

empty.doc: Shown when the launcher has no circles.

loading.doc: Shown while circles are being fetched.

back.doc: Back link from a circle detail view to the launcher.

detail_empty.doc: Empty state inside a circle detail view.

on_your_list.doc: Heading for the claim-router mirror section on CircleDetail (P6.5 #342).

title.doc: Heading of the cross-circle Stream tab (board 5B).

open.doc: Launcher entry that opens the cross-circle Stream.

untagged.doc: Tag shown on a Stream row that isn't tied to a circle.

compose_placeholder_default.doc: Pinned compose placeholder when no row is focused (P6.M3).

compose_placeholder_targeted.doc: Pinned compose placeholder when a row is focused — context follows.

fallback_label.doc: α.5a (audit #3) — default label for an inline-keuze quick-reply pill when the reply envelope supplies a slash but no label.

pending.doc: δ.2 — bubble icon caption while the kring fan-out (broadcastKringMessage) is in flight.

sent.doc: δ.2 — bubble icon caption after a successful kring fan-out.  Currently never surfaced (the happy path renders nothing) but reserved for future use.

failed.doc: δ.2 — bubble icon caption + accessible label when the kring fan-out rejected or returned errors; tapping re-fires broadcastKringMessage with the same msgId.

requesting.doc: ε.5 — chat-shell pill while a negotiated catch-up is in flight (PENDING_OFFERS / collecting-offers phase).

streaming_progress.doc: ε.5 — chat-shell pill during the streaming phase; count is items ingested so far, total is the offer's count.

done.doc: ε.5 — terminal pill shown briefly after a successful catch-up; auto-hides after 1.5s.

no_offers.text: No-one online to catch up from
no_offers.doc: ε.5 — terminal pill when the offer window elapsed without any peer replying.

provider_request_title.doc: ε.5 — provider-side notification card title when an inbound catch-up-request needs manual approval.

provider_request_size.doc: ε.5 — provider-side notification card size summary.

provider_decline.doc: ε.5 — provider-side decline action; silent on the wire (no envelope sent to the receiver).

chooser_title.doc: ε.6 — multi-offer chooser modal heading (kring's catchUpChooserMode='prompt').

chooser_subtitle.doc: ε.6 — chooser modal subheading; count is offers collected.

chooser_msg_count.doc: ε.6 — per-offer card: how many messages the source is offering.

chooser_size_kb.doc: ε.6 — per-offer card: total size estimate.

chooser_recent.doc: ε.6 — per-offer card: relative time of the newest offered message.

chooser_all.doc: ε.6 — per-offer accept button: send everything since sinceTs.

chooser_last_50.doc: ε.6 — per-offer accept button: tail 50 messages.

chooser_last_7d.doc: ε.6 — per-offer accept button: messages from the last 7 days.

chooser_cancel.doc: ε.6 — chooser modal global cancel; declines all offers (silent on the wire).

chooser_unknown_provider.doc: ε.6 — fallback name when resolveContact returns null AND the offerFrom addr is empty.

gesprek.doc: SP-13.3 — kring bottom-tab label (board Voorbeeld 1-3, always first).

prikbord.doc: SP-13.3 — bottom-tab when policy.features.noticeboard is on.

taken.doc: SP-13.3 — bottom-tab when tasks is on.

lijsten.doc: SP-13.3 — bottom-tab when lists is on.

notities.doc: SP-13.3 — bottom-tab when notes is on.

agenda.doc: SP-13.3 — bottom-tab when calendar is on.

leden.doc: SP-13.3 — bottom-tab when memberDirectory is on (renders last per board ordering).

more.doc: SP-13 — kring header overflow menu trigger.

empty.text: Nothing here yet — be the first to write.
empty.doc: SP-13.2 — empty state for the kring's chat-style GESPREK view.

composer_placeholder.doc: SP-13.2 — inline composer placeholder; hosts can pass a kring-specific one (e.g. 'Schrijf naar de buurt…').

send.doc: SP-13.2 — composer send button a11y label (visual is an ↑ glyph).

tab_coming.text: {{tab}} content lands in a follow-up slice.
tab_coming.doc: SP-13.3 — placeholder body for a tab whose content surface isn't built yet (e.g. PRIKBORD / LEDEN).

tabs_label.doc: SP-13.3 — a11y label on the per-kring bottom tab bar.

view_chat.doc: SP-13.4 — Chat ↔ Scherm header toggle, chat-mode label (v2 §4).

view_scherm.doc: SP-13.4 — Chat ↔ Scherm toggle, scherm-mode label.

view_toggle_label.text: Switch between chat and screen view
view_toggle_label.doc: SP-13.4 — a11y group label on the Chat | Scherm pill.

scherm_coming.text: Screen view (admin's recept'd page) lands in a follow-up slice.
scherm_coming.doc: SP-13.4 — placeholder until the recept renderer is built.

empty.text: The admin hasn't set up a screen yet.
empty.doc: α.1c — shown when the kring's recipe book is empty.

loading.doc: Shown while materializeRecipe/materializeScreen is still resolving — distinct from the empty state which was previously misused for both.

refreshing.doc: δ.1 — subtle pip tooltip while a cached screen is rendered and a fresh materialize runs in the background.

block_empty.doc: α.1c — per-block empty fallback when a block has no content.

block_error.doc: α.1c — per-block error fallback when materialization throws.

block_unknown.doc: α.1c — defensive label for a block type the renderer doesn't recognize (forward-compat).

no_screens.text: No screens yet. Add one below.

untitled.doc: α.3 — placeholder for screens with no name.

active.doc: α.3 — badge on the active screen.

seed_name.doc: α.3 — name of the auto-seeded default screen.

seed_my_things.doc: α.4 — seeded screen aggregating tasks assigned to me across kringen.

seed_my_calendar.doc: α.4 — seeded screen aggregating upcoming events across kringen.

quickActions.doc: D1 §5A — block palette label for the Veel-gebruikt action row.

no_recipes.text: No screens yet. Add one below.

untitled.doc: α.1d — placeholder when a recipe has no name.

active.doc: α.1d — badge on the currently-active recipe.

back_to_book.doc: α.1d — go from recipe edit to book.

no_blocks.text: No blocks yet. Pick one below.

rules_hint.text: Renders the kring's current house rules.
rules_hint.doc: α.1d — rules block has no config.

label.doc: α.5c — checkbox label for the compact-rows toggle on list-shaped blocks.

hint.doc: α.5c — hint under the Compact checkbox.

title.text: Two of you edited this recipe
title.doc: γ.3 — modal heading when an incoming recipe diverges from local.

instructions.text: Pick which version to keep for each item.
instructions.doc: γ.3 — modal subhead explaining the picker.

block_label.doc: γ.3 — per-block row label; {{name}} is emoji + block type label.

meta_label.doc: γ.3 — meta-conflict row label; {{path}} is the dotted path.

meta_section.doc: γ.3 — section header above the meta-conflict list.

keep_yours.doc: γ.3 — picker choice: keep local version.

take_theirs.doc: γ.3 — picker choice: take incoming version.

keep_both.doc: γ.3 — picker choice (blocks only): keep local + add incoming with a fresh id.

apply.doc: γ.3 — confirm picks + save the merged recipe.

cancel.doc: γ.3 — discard incoming; keep local as-is.

help.doc: Stream row action — accept a question (P6.M3).

offer.doc: Stream row action — offer to lend.

take.doc: Stream row action — accept an offer.

claim.doc: Stream row action — claim a chore.

done.doc: Stream row action — mark a reminder done.

ignore.doc: Stream row action — dismiss without acting.

features.doc: Settings axis: which features the circle has.

agents.doc: Settings axis: whether LLM agents may join.

revealPolicy.doc: Settings axis: who sees real names.

consensus.doc: Settings section: co-admin approval for changes.

consequence_aria.doc: Accessible label for the per-option ⓘ consequence toggle.

off.text: No AI assistance in this circle — nothing is sent to any model.

local.text: AI runs on a member's own machine (Ollama). Messages stay on-device; nothing goes to the cloud.

cloud.text: AI runs on an independent cloud model. Your messages are sent to that provider when you ask it something.

yes.text: Anyone can add an AI agent as a participant. Agents can read whatever the circle shares.

admin-approval.text: AI agents may join only after an admin approves — a checkpoint before any agent sees circle content.

no.text: No AI agents may join. Only people take part in this circle.

pairwise.text: Real names are revealed one-to-one. Each member chooses who gets to see their identity.

open.text: Every member can see each other's real names. There is no per-person hiding.

none.text: Nothing is stored to a pod. Data lives only on members' devices and is lost if they clear it.

shared.text: One shared pod holds the circle's data — every member reads and writes the same store.

personal.text: Each member keeps their own pod and the circle links to them by reference. You keep control of your own copy.

hybrid.text: A shared pod plus personal pods: common items are shared, private items stay with each member.

send_proposal.doc: Save-button label when consensus is required.

pending.text: Changes need co-admin approval before they apply.
pending.doc: Footer note when consensus is active.

pending_waiting.doc: Footer note when a proposal is outstanding; {{who}} is the comma-separated approver list.

title.text: Two of you edited the settings
title.doc: γ.4 — modal heading when an incoming policy diverges from local; every other resolver locale string is reused from circle.recipe.conflict.*.

storagePosture.doc: Circle settings — at-rest storage posture axis (menukaart P0–P3).

chatOff.doc: Override: silence this circle's chat for me.

revealOpen.text: Show my name to all members
revealOpen.doc: Override: reveal my real name circle-wide.

agentsMayContactMe.doc: Override: allow LLM agents to address me.

push.doc: Override section: split push toggles (P6.M4 · board 6A).

pushOnMention.doc: Push when someone @mentions me — default on.

pushOnEveryMessage.doc: Push on every message in this circle — default off.

flowThrough.doc: Override section: route circle items into my personal circle.

tasksToPersonal.doc: Override: claimed circle tasks land in my personal list.

calendarToPersonal.doc: Override: circle events also show in my calendar.

section_title.doc: α.5b · audit #6 — heading of the per-kring push-toggles block on the personal override sheet (board 6A).

on_mention.doc: α.5b — push toggle: notify me when someone @-mentions me in this circle (default on).

on_message.doc: α.5b — push toggle: notify me for every new chat message in this circle (default off — noisy).

on_new_item.doc: α.5b — push toggle: notify me when a new noticeboard / agenda / task / announcement item is added in this circle (default on).

on_proposal.doc: α.5b — push toggle: notify me when a multi-admin voorstel (P6.2) is created in this circle (default on).

holiday.doc: Availability section: away until a date.

edit_end.doc: Opens the date picker for an absolute end date.

quietHours.doc: Availability section: defer pushes in a window.

autoReply.doc: Outgoing auto-reply shown to peers who try to reach me during holiday (board 6C · P6.M5).

autoReplyAnon.doc: Anon form when name isn't resolvable.

autoReplyNoEnd.doc: When holiday has no end date.

title.doc: Heading of the View-as reveal preview (board 4C).

agent.doc: View-as viewer option: an LLM agent.

revealed.doc: Badge: this member's real name is visible to the chosen viewer.

hidden.doc: Badge: this member shows only a handle to the chosen viewer.

too_busy_btn.doc: Advisor: member logs a 'too busy' strain signal.

too_busy_logged.doc: Confirmation after logging a too-busy signal.

advice_too_busy.text: This circle has had {{count}} 'too busy' signals in two weeks while it's getting busier. Consider sharing the load — add a co-admin or split off a sub-circle.
advice_too_busy.doc: Advice card for the too-busy + growth rule; {{count}} interpolated.

global_label.text: Let trusted contacts relay my requests one hop

explain.text: When you ask for something no one nearby has, a contact who allows it can pass your request one step further — to their contacts. Max one hop, and the relay is anonymized.

ask_relay.doc: Button: ask the gate contact to relay the request one hop.

over_limit.text: Too many hops — requests travel at most one step.
over_limit.doc: Shown when a chain exceeds max-one-hop.

default_label.doc: Chip shown when the per-contact mode falls through to the global stance.

no_matches.doc: Empty state for the skill match list.

openness.doc: Skill axis: how openly it is shared.

posture.doc: Skill axis: fixed vs negotiable offer.

radius.doc: Skill axis: how far it reaches.

agent.doc: Match source badge: an LLM agent.

via-hop.doc: Match source badge: reached via a one-hop relay.

empty.text: No files in this circle yet.
empty.doc: Empty state for the circle file browser.

empty_folder.doc: N5 — empty state when a folder has no subfolders or files.

root.doc: N5 — root breadcrumb label for the Folio folder tree.

folder_count.doc: N5 — file count badge on a folder row; {{count}} interpolated.

source_index.doc: N5 — Folio source toggle: the in-app file index.

source_pod.doc: N5 — Folio source toggle: the user's real signed-in pod.

connect_pod.text: Connect your pod to browse your files here.
connect_pod.doc: N5 — shown when the pod source is picked but no pod is signed in.

my_things_title.doc: Board 10A heading (P6.M7) — Folio shown as the private kring.

my_things_empty.text: Nothing here yet — items you keep to yourself land here.
my_things_empty.doc: Empty state on the My-things notes-list.

shared_by_me.doc: Board 10B filter (P6.M8) — items I own, scoped to a circle.

shared_with_me.doc: Board 10B filter (P6.M8) — items others shared into a circle I'm in.

shared_by_me_empty.text: You haven't shared any items yet.

shared_with_me_empty.text: Nobody has shared an item with you yet.
shared_with_me_empty.doc: Empty state for the Shared-with-me filter.

title.doc: Heading of the passive PoL row on the circle detail (board 10C, slice 5.9d).

notConfigured.doc: Shown when no attestation reader is wired.

attestedAt.doc: Shown when a reader returns a populated status.

unavailable.doc: Board 5C notice (P6.4) shown in the chat-compose area when the recipient has chat-off for this circle.

unavailable_anon.text: This person doesn't receive chat in this circle.
unavailable_anon.doc: Same notice when recipient/circle names aren't resolvable; never leaks an id.

save.doc: Compose-area CTA to queue the message for when the recipient re-enables chat.

withdraw.doc: Compose-area CTA to discard the typed message.

no_delivery_note.doc: Secondary line clarifying the non-event per board 5C (no "tried to reach you" leak).

title.doc: Board 7A auto-hop-prompt card heading (P6.6) when an in-circle skill search returns nothing.

body.text: No one in your circles matches "{{skill}}". Want me to search via your {{count}} eligible contact(s)?
body.doc: Card body with the user's query interpolated.

body_anon.text: No one in your circles matches that. Want me to search via your {{count}} eligible contact(s)?
body_anon.doc: Anon body when the query string can't be safely echoed.

accept.doc: Primary CTA — triggers the hop-relay request.

dismiss.doc: Secondary CTA — records dismissal so we don't re-prompt the same query.

title.doc: Heading for the inline skill-match list (P6.7 #345) appended under /find replies.
Board 8C — heading of the dedicated `HIER` screen (P6.8).

header.doc: Header line above the row list.

header_empty.doc: Shown when no peers are visible on the local network.

anon_peer.doc: Fallback row label when a peer has no derivable pseudonym.

own_profile.doc: Footer block heading; shows your own pseudonym + published-tier skills.

own_profile_empty.text: You haven't published any skills yet.
own_profile_empty.doc: Footer when no skills are published.

sees_anon.doc: Anon form when viewer name is missing.

up_empty.text: No migration needed yet — there's nothing to move.

up_with_content.text: Existing content will be migrated to the new storage choice.

down_with_content.text: Going back consolidates content — items that don't fit will be merged or lost. Ask the other admin first.
down_with_content.doc: Going down to a non-none level (severity=warn).

down_to_none.text: You can't move back to "no pod" once content lives in a pod. Pick the other admin's flow if you really need to start over.
down_to_none.doc: Going all the way down (severity=block, allowed=false).

title.doc: Board 4B card heading on the admin's inbox (P6.10).

title_anon.text: An agent is waiting for admin approval
title_anon.doc: Anon form when the requester's name isn't resolvable.

who.doc: Label for the agent's identity row.

whoBody.doc: Agent name + kind in the body.

reject.doc: Secondary CTA — flips the request to rejected on a single click.

ask.doc: Tertiary CTA — opens a DM to the requester to clarify.

pending.doc: Status line shown to the requester / other admins while approvals are outstanding.

blocked.doc: Shown when the agents axis is `no`.

required_note.text: Add a purpose and the agreements to save.
required_note.doc: Shown when required rules fields are blank.

consent_empty.text: This circle hasn't written its rules yet.
consent_empty.doc: Consent screen with an empty rules doc.

admins.text: Who runs it, and what can they decide on their own?

conflict.text: When people disagree, how do you sort it out?

admission.text: How does someone new get in?

leaving.text: When someone leaves, what happens to their stuff and tasks?

responsibility.doc: Rules field: responsibility (folded into agreements; shown if filled).

purpose.doc: α.1c — rules block short field label.

agreements.doc: α.1c — rules block short field label.

title.text: Two of you edited the rules
title.doc: γ.4 — modal heading when an incoming rules doc diverges from local; every other resolver locale string is reused from circle.recipe.conflict.*.

applied_hint.doc: β.4 — confirmation shown after picking a kind in the C1 create-circle wizard; the matching kringTemplate has just pre-filled the policy axes. {{kind}} is the picked kind id (household / buurt / vriendenkring / team).

kindPicker.doc: N1+E8 — kind picker legend in the create wizard.

label.text: How big will this buurt be?
label.doc: N1 — buurt size question driving the chat advice.

chatToggle.doc: N1 — the chat-feature toggle in the create wizard.

buurtLarge.text: For a larger buurt we recommend keeping open chat OFF — a busy all-to-all chat overwhelms people. Reactions to a notice still spin up a focused thread.
buurtLarge.doc: N1 — advice for a large buurt (chat off).

buurtSmall.text: For a small buurt you can go either way — open chat can work. By default it stays off (noticeboard-first); turn it on if your group wants it.
buurtSmall.doc: N1 — advice for a small buurt (ask).

buurtDefault.text: Buurten start noticeboard-first: open chat is off, and a thread appears only when someone reacts to a vraag or aanbod.
buurtDefault.doc: N1 — neutral buurt chat default note.

which.doc: Circle bot asks which item, when a free-text command matches MULTIPLE items in the circle (clarification turn).

pick.doc: Button label on each candidate in the clarification list.

notFound.text: I couldn’t find “{{query}}” in this circle.
notFound.doc: Circle bot reply when a free-text command target matches NO item in the circle.

ok.doc: Generic success indicator rendered when a skill returns { ok: true } with no message.

failed.doc: Generic failure fallback when a skill returns { ok: false } with no error text.

error.doc: Generic error label when an error envelope has no message or code.

consequences.doc: N2 — ⓘ tooltip/aria label on a radio option that reveals its consequence.

unknown_command.text: Didn't understand “{{input}}”. Try /help for the commands available here.
unknown_command.doc: Shown when parser falls through to kind:'unknown' — user input matched no slash command.

bulk_no_list.text: No recent list to apply “all” to — run a list command first.
bulk_no_list.doc: E2 — shown when `/<op> all` has no recent listing to fan out over.

stale_list.text: This list is stale. Run the command again to refresh.
stale_list.doc: Shown when the user taps a disabled action menu (per A2 hybrid lifecycle).

welcome.text: Welcome to canopy-chat (v0.2). Try /help. Create more threads via the sidebar.
welcome.doc: First-boot greeting in the Main thread.

permission_no_commands.text: This thread does not accept commands.
permission_no_commands.doc: Shown when allowCommands=false on the active thread.

no_peer.text: DM thread has no paired peer address.
no_peer.doc: Shown when a DM-scoped thread is missing its actor address (config bug).

peer_offline.text: Peer transport not connected. Try /peer-connect first.
peer_offline.doc: Shown when the user sends in a DM but NKN isn't connected.

respond_neither_ok.text: Neither local audit nor NKN delivery succeeded.
respond_neither_ok.doc: [Help with] flow — both substrate and NKN paths failed.

counter_prompt.doc: Hint after [Counter] click on a responder card.

heading.doc: First line of /help output; precedes the per-app command sections.

section_chat.doc: Section heading for chat-shell built-in commands (/help, /brief, /threads).

section_app.doc: Section heading for an app's commands. {{app}} = app id (household, tasks-v0, …).

empty.doc: Shown when /help fires before any app manifests have loaded.

heading.doc: Form heading; opId is the manifest op.

cancelled.doc: Shown after the user clicks [Cancel] on a form.

no_callskill.text: Embed dispatch not available in this build.

no_factory.text: No app declares a Q29 cardSnapshotSkill yet.
no_factory.doc: When the merged catalog has no embed-producing op.

no_input.doc: When /embed-file fires with no recognisable input.

pick_unavailable.text: File picker not available in this build.

no_title.text: Please provide a title for the appointment.

no_when.text: Please provide a when date for the appointment.

bad_when.text: Couldn't parse "{{when}}" as a date. Try YYYY-MM-DD or 'tomorrow 3pm'.

stub.text: [demo] {{op}} clicked on {{item}} — backing app not built yet (tracked).
stub.doc: Receiver-action stub on file/time cards before real backing apps exist.

no_runner.text: Brief aggregator not available in this build.
Search not available in this build.

no_log.text: Event log not available in this build.

panel_opened.text: Logs panel opened. Click [×] to close.
panel_opened.doc: Confirmation when bare /logs opens the side-panel UI.

not_available.text: QR scanner not available on this platform (mobile only today).
not_available.doc: Web doesn't ship a webcam scanner yet; /scan-qr returns this hint.

scanned_contact.doc: Bot bubble after scanning a stoop-contact:// QR and addContactFromQr succeeds.

scanned_invite.text: Opening join-buurt wizard with scanned invite…
scanned_invite.doc: Bot bubble after scanning a stoop-invite://; the wizard takes over.

scan_failed.doc: Substrate returned error or classifier saw an unknown payload.

scan_unknown.text: Unrecognised QR code. Expected a stoop-contact:// or stoop-invite:// URL.

no_flow.text: Sign-in flow not available in this build.
no_flow.doc: Defensive — neither podAuth nor externalFlow wired.

opening.text: Opening sign-in (issuer: {{issuer}}) … come back when the redirect lands.

unknown_issuer.text: Unknown issuer "{{issuer}}". Try 'inrupt', 'solidcommunity', 'solidweb' or a full URL.

completed.doc: Shown when the external callback resumes the chat.

welcome.doc: v0.7.P1 — OIDC round-trip completed on boot.

unavailable.text: Identity check is not available in this build.
Identity rotation not available in this build.
Crypto status not available in this build.
Mute not available in this build.

added.text:
🔇 Muted {{peer}}.
Their messages will be dropped + you can't send to them.

unavailable.text: Audit log not available in this build.
Debug dump not available — secure-agent factory not wired.

no_store.text: Thread store not available in this build.

downloaded.text: ↓ Downloaded {{name}} to your browser's default download location.

no_picker.text: File picker not available in this build.

no_lookup.text: Peer-resolution unavailable; sign in to your pod.

lookup_failed.text: Couldn't resolve {{peer}} to an NKN address.

too_large.text: File too large ({{size}} bytes; max {{max}} bytes inline).  NKN drops messages over ~64KB silently, so we cap at 32KB to guarantee delivery.  Larger files need pod-URL hand-off (next slice).

unavailable.text: Peer lookup unavailable (sign in to your pod first).

not_found.text: No NKN address found for {{webid}}.  They may not have signed in to canopy-chat from a pod yet.

unavailable.text: Peer transport not available in this build.

no_address.text: Please provide a peer NKN address: /test-peer <addr> [text]

not_connected.text: Not connected to NKN yet.  Try /peer-connect (and wait 5-90s).
not_connected.doc: Defensive: NKN not connected when sending.

unavailable.text: Sign-out is not available in this build.

no_store.text: Thread store not available in this build.

empty.doc: When no apps in the catalog.

no_registry.text: App registry not available in this build.

no_name.doc: When toggle action has no app name.

unknown_peer.doc: When the peer name isn't in simPeers.

no_thread.text: Peer's thread (id={{threadId}}) does not exist in this workspace.

sent.doc: Confirmation in the sender's thread after /send-to.

synced_to.doc: Decentralized sync hint suffix; ok=confirmed, total=peers.

pending.doc: Decentralized sync hint suffix; count of peers awaiting confirmation.

unreachable.doc: Decentralized sync hint suffix; list of unreachable peer webids.

podless.doc: Pod-less sync hint suffix; shows the oldest last-seen time.

podless_empty.doc: Pod-less sync hint when no peers known.

saved_locally.doc: Decentralized empty-state suffix (per OQ-6.A): all peers offline / no peers known yet.

no_name.doc: When /newthread fires with no name arg.

no_store.text: Thread workspace not available in this build.
no_store.doc: Defensive — should never trigger in the standard web build.

no_store.text: Thread workspace not available in this build.

heading.doc: Sidebar heading above the thread list.

new_thread.doc: Button that toggles the new-thread form.

edit.doc: Per-thread edit button tooltip — opens the new-thread form pre-filled.

form_create_heading.doc: Title shown above the form in create mode.

form_edit_heading.doc: Title shown above the form in edit mode.

form_apps.doc: New-thread form: apps filter chip-group label.

form_apps_hint.text: Pick which apps' events appear here. Empty = all apps.
form_apps_hint.doc: Help text under the apps chip group.

form_add_custom_app.doc: Placeholder for the custom-app input below the chip group.

form_event_types_hint.text: Pick which kinds of events trigger this thread. Empty = all kinds.
form_event_types_hint.doc: Help text under the event-types chip group.

form_add_custom_type.doc: Placeholder for the custom-event-type input below the chip group.

form_peers.doc: Field label for the peers CSV input.

form_peers_hint.text: Restrict this thread to events from named peers. Empty = anyone.
form_peers_hint.doc: Help text under the peers field.

dm_unknown.doc: Placeholder DM peer label shown before identity is exchanged (Slice 6a).

known.doc: Trust level for an acknowledged contact. Stoop persists this as Dutch 'bekend'; chat-shell translates EN at the boundary.

trusted.doc: Trust level for a fully-trusted contact. Stoop persists this as Dutch 'vertrouwd'; chat-shell translates EN at the boundary.

no_substrate.text: Relay support not wired in this build.
no_substrate.doc: /set-relay called but agent.relay isn't exposed (test workspace or old build).

url_required.text: Relay URL required. Try /set-relay ws://your-host:port or /set-relay --clear

bad_url.text: Bad relay URL. Must start with ws:// or wss://
bad_url.doc: /set-relay called with a URL that doesn't have a websocket scheme.

settings_hint.text: Adjust your preferences. Changes apply immediately.

no_substrate.text: Transport-mode control not wired in this build.
no_substrate.doc: /transport-mode or /transports called but agent.setTransportMode isn't exposed.

bad_mode.text: Bad mode "{{mode}}". Pick nkn, relay, or both.

mode_set.text: ✓ Transport mode set: {{mode}}. Outbound peer sends now route this way.

open.doc: E1 — file-reply card: open the file URL in a new tab.

unnamed.doc: E1 — file-reply card fallback name when none is given.

backToChat.doc: E4 — chat-nav floating button: return to the thread a side-panel was opened from.

openInFull.doc: E5 — record/mini-page panel: expand the card into the wide side panel.

invite-only.text: Only people an admin invites can join. Tightest control; nobody slips in via a shared link.
invite-only.doc: N2 — consequence note for a radio option (Gevolgen als je dit kiest).

request.text: Anyone can ask to join, but an admin must approve each request. A middle ground.
request.doc: N2 — consequence note for a radio option (Gevolgen als je dit kiest).

open.text: Anyone who knows the buurt id joins instantly — no gate. Easiest to grow, hardest to keep private.
open.doc: N2 — consequence note for a radio option (Gevolgen als je dit kiest).

anyone.text: Members can leave whenever they want, silently. Lowest friction; you may not notice departures.
anyone.doc: N2 — consequence note for a radio option (Gevolgen als je dit kiest).

notify-first.text: Leavers post a notice before going, so the buurt sees who left and why.
notify-first.doc: N2 — consequence note for a radio option (Gevolgen als je dit kiest).

admin-decides.text: Disputes go to an admin who rules. Fast, but concentrates power.
admin-decides.doc: N2 — consequence note for a radio option (Gevolgen als je dit kiest).

mediation.text: Two random members mediate. More neutral, but slower and needs willing volunteers.
mediation.doc: N2 — consequence note for a radio option (Gevolgen als je dit kiest).

vote.text: The whole buurt votes. Most democratic, but heavy for small disputes.
vote.doc: N2 — consequence note for a radio option (Gevolgen als je dit kiest).

no-pod.text: Everything lives in local app state only. Simplest to start; nothing syncs across your devices and there is no backup.
no-pod.doc: N2 — consequence note for a radio option (Gevolgen als je dit kiest).

decentralised.text: Each member keeps their own pod and they sync. Resilient + private, but every member needs a pod set up.
decentralised.doc: N2 — consequence note for a radio option (Gevolgen als je dit kiest).

centralised.text: One shared group pod holds everything (you provide its URI). Easy to back up, but one party hosts the data.
centralised.doc: N2 — consequence note for a radio option (Gevolgen als je dit kiest).

household.text: A home: everyone sees each other, one shared pod, light governance. Chat on by default.
household.doc: N2 — consequence note for a radio option (Gevolgen als je dit kiest).

buurt.text: A neighbourhood: pairwise reveal, per-member pods, co-admin consensus. Noticeboard-first — open chat off by default.
buurt.doc: N2 — consequence note for a radio option (Gevolgen als je dit kiest).

vriendenkring.text: A friend group: relaxed, open reveal, no house rules or member directory. Chat on by default.
vriendenkring.doc: N2 — consequence note for a radio option (Gevolgen als je dit kiest).

team.text: A workspace: cloud LLM + agents on, shared pod, no noticeboard. Chat on by default.
team.doc: N2 — consequence note for a radio option (Gevolgen als je dit kiest).

small.text: A few households. Open chat can work — the wizard leaves the choice to you (default off).
small.doc: N2 — consequence note for a radio option (Gevolgen als je dit kiest).

large.text: A whole neighbourhood. We advise keeping open chat off — an all-to-all chat overwhelms; reactions still start focused threads.
large.doc: N2 — consequence note for a radio option (Gevolgen als je dit kiest).

private.text: Only you see this skill. Nothing is offered to anyone — useful while you decide.
private.doc: N2.b — consequence note for a radio option.

circle.text: Members of this circle can see + ask. Stays inside the circle.
circle.doc: N2.b — consequence note for a radio option.

contacts.text: Your direct contacts can see it too, across circles. Wider reach, still people you know.
contacts.doc: N2.b — consequence note for a radio option.

public.text: Anyone who can find your profile sees it. Maximum reach, least control.
public.doc: N2.b — consequence note for a radio option.

always.text: You commit to it up front — askers can count on it without negotiating.
always.doc: N2.b — consequence note for a radio option.

negotiable.text: Each request is a conversation; you say yes/no per case. More flexible, more back-and-forth.
negotiable.doc: N2.b — consequence note for a radio option.

active.text: Live and matchable now — it shows up in searches + skill matches.
active.doc: N2.b — consequence note for a radio option.

paused.text: Hidden from matches for now, but kept. Flip back to active anytime.
paused.doc: N2.b — consequence note for a radio option.

archived.text: Retired. Out of all matches; keep it as a record without deleting.
archived.doc: N2.b — consequence note for a radio option.

home.text: Stays within your household. The smallest reach.
home.doc: N2.b — consequence note for a radio option.

street.doc: N2.b — consequence note for a radio option.

neighbourhood.doc: N2.b — consequence note for a radio option.

city.text: Reaches the wider city. Largest reach, least local.
city.doc: N2.b — consequence note for a radio option.

extraRolesHint.text: A circle starts with just admins + members. Add a starter role only if you need one — you can change this later.

desc.text: A short visit — sees the circle but posts or does nothing. Good for a one-off look.
Follows along without taking part. E.g. a new member finding their feet, or a researcher.
Helps from outside with specific, time-boxed tasks. Limited access; not a full member.
